title: Redis state store: queryIndexes init fails on Redis 8.8+ ("SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists")
body:
## Expected Behavior

The `state.redis` component with `queryIndexes` should initialize successfully when the query index already exists in Redis, taking the existing drop-and-recreate path. This is what happens on Redis < 8.8, and it is what makes running more than one replica of an app (or restarting a single replica) possible at all, since every daprd instance runs `FT.CREATE` for the configured indexes at init.

## Actual Behavior

On **Redis 8.8+**, component init fails and daprd exits whenever the index already exists — i.e. for every replica after the first, and for any pod restart:

```
level=fatal msg="Fatal error from runtime: process component teststore error: [INIT_COMPONENT_FAILURE]:
initialization error occurred for teststore (state.redis/v1): redis store: error registering query schemas:
SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists"
```

Root cause: Redis 8.8 started prefixing module error replies with an error code. A duplicate `FT.CREATE` now returns `SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists` instead of `Index already exists`:

```
$ docker run --rm -d --name r redis:8.6.3   # ... FT.CREATE twice -> "Index already exists"
$ docker run --rm -d --name r redis:8.8.0   # ... FT.CREATE twice -> "SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists"
```

`registerSchemas` in `state/redis/redis.go` compares the error string exactly, so the new format is treated as a fatal error instead of taking the drop-and-recreate path:

```go
if err.Error() != "Index already exists" {
    return err
}
```

Verified against daprd 1.16.4 and 1.18.0 (components-contrib v1.18.0 and current master have the same comparison).

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

1. Start Redis 8.8: `docker network create t && docker run -d --name redis88 --network t redis:8.8.0`
2. Create a `state.redis` component with `queryIndexes`:
   ```yaml
   apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
   kind: Component
   metadata:
     name: teststore
   spec:
     type: state.redis
     version: v1
     metadata:
       - name: redisHost
         value: redis88:6379
       - name: queryIndexes
         value: |
           [{"name":"userIdx","indexes":[{"key":"user.email","type":"TEXT"}]}]
   ```
3. Run daprd once — index is created, component loads fine.
4. Run daprd again (simulates a second replica or a pod restart): `docker run --name daprd --network t -v $PWD/components:/components daprio/daprd:1.18.0 ./daprd --app-id test --resources-path /components`
5. daprd exits with `INIT_COMPONENT_FAILURE ... SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists`.

Repeating the same steps with `redis:8.6.3` works (logs show `drop stale query index` and the component loads).

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Redis state store query index initialization on Redis 8.8+ ("SEARCH_INDEX_EXISTS Index already exists" error on component init).
